symptoms: Failure correlated with large input size
summary.expected_behavior: Application should validate input size and handle large inputs gracefully
summary.actual_behavior: Application crashes when processing large input payloads

analysis.error_message: IndexError: list assignment index out of range
stack_trace_excerpt: ERROR 2026-04-08 10:12:01 ValueError: Input size exceeds allocated buffer capacity

execution_output.stderr:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Users\parkavi.r\Downloads\assessment_2_bug_debug\repro\repro_script.py", line 9, in <module>
    process_input(large_input)
  File "C:\Users\parkavi.r\Downloads\assessment_2_bug_debug\repro\repro_script.py", line 5, in process_input
    buffer[i] = data[i]
    ~~~~~~^^^
IndexError: list assignment index out of range

analysis.root_cause: The application writes input data into a fixed-size buffer without validating input length, leading to an out-of-bounds write.
patch_plan: Add explicit input size validation before buffer write operations.
Reject or truncate inputs that exceed buffer capacity.
Add unit tests covering boundary and oversized inputs.
validation_plan: Re-run the minimal reproduction script to confirm the crash no longer occurs.
Add automated tests for maximum allowed input sizes.
Perform regression testing on existing input scenarios.
risks: Input truncation may impact downstream processing logic.
Strict validation may change behavior for existing users.

critiques: The reproduction successfully triggers the crash, but it assumes a fixed buffer size without verifying real production constraints.
The root cause assumes missing input validation is the sole issue. Other contributors such as memory reuse or concurrent access were not evaluated.
The proposed fix plan is reasonable but may introduce behavior changes for clients relying on current lax validation.
open_questions: What is the intended maximum input size according to product requirements?
Are there additional call paths where large input can bypass validation?
Is input size enforcement consistent across all services handling this data?
